summary: First testing pass on PitchFinder. Backend: 23/23 pytest cases pass (auth, users, groups, membership, messages, matches). Frontend: mobile Expo web preview fully functional — onboarding (3 slides + Se connecter), sign-in with demo creds, home feed with greeting/CTA/group cards, group detail with join, chat send/receive, discover with search+level chips+sort chips, chat tab listing joined groups, profile with stats/badges, edit profile save via /users/me, and signout returning to onboarding. No deprecated Expo packages detected (no expo-av/expo-barcode-scanner/expo-background-fetch). No red screens. One non-blocking React Native Web warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'.

issues: Group cards without a photo (e.g. newly-created TEST groups) display as flat dark rectangles with no placeholder image — consider a fallback illustration for polish

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Likely coming from a nested library component. Non-blocking but should be traced (search for pointerEvents= props in codebase / dep versions).

action_items: Optional polish: add a placeholder/gradient banner for groups without a photo on Home and Discover cards.
Investigate 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' warning — likely in a library or a custom component; migrate to style.pointerEvents.

Consider cleaning up test-generated groups (TEST_*) that pytest creates — the seed function is idempotent so they persist between runs, polluting Discover. Suggest either a teardown fixture on the backend side or a dedicated /api/test/cleanup endpoint (dev-only).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 790-line file — acceptable for MVP but consider splitting into routers/ (auth, groups, messages, matches) as the app grows.

group_to_public uses `hash(g['id']) % 25` as distance fallback which changes across Python processes (PYTHONHASHSEED). Since seed data provides distance_km explicitly, this fallback rarely fires, but consider a deterministic default.
MongoDB _id is properly excluded from all responses via `{'_id': 0}` projections — good.
JWT secret is set via env with a hard-coded dev fallback — acceptable for MVP; the .env value is strong.
No pagination on /api/groups (limit=200) or /api/groups/{id}/messages (limit=500) — fine for MVP scale, should be added before scaling.
seed_data_creation: None manually created. Pytest run POSTed a few TEST_ groups via /api/groups (creator=demo user). These remain in DB and appear in Discover as 'TEST Group xxxxxx'. Not harmful, but persists.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 23 backend pytest cases pass against the public URL. Frontend flows all validated on 390x844 mobile viewport with mobile Chrome via Playwright. Login prefill works with demo@pitchfinder.app / demo1234. Key testIDs used and confirmed present: signin-email-input, signin-password-input, signin-submit-button, home-screen, tab-icon-{index,discover,chat,profile}, group-card-{id}, group-join-button, chat-input, chat-send-button, discover-search-input, edit-profile-button, edit-name-input, edit-save-button, signout-button. Note: Pydantic EmailStr in FastAPI rejects reserved TLDs like .test — use @example.com for test emails. Pre-existing TEST groups appear in Discover due to no cleanup logic.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no blocking issues found. Only fix applied: replaced .test TLD with example.com in pytest fixtures because Pydantic v2 EmailStr treats .test as reserved and returns 422; this was a test-side issue, not app code.
